title: Template production for mRNA in vitro transcription by PCR
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"108pd","text":"This a protocol for producing a linear, dsDNA amplicon with KOD hot-start high-fidelity DNA polymerase for subsequent use in in vitro transcription of mRNA using SP6 RNA polymerase. It includes removal of the circular, dam-methylated plasmid DNA template via DpnI digestion and instructions for purification and concentration of the final DNA. A single reaction should yield 1 to 5 µg DNA and the protocol can easily scaled up to produce larger amounts of high-quality, in vitro transcription-ready DNA.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":125,"length":8},{"style":"italic","offset":259,"length":3},{"style":"italic","offset":470,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"7anj9","text":"The RNA polymerases commonly used in in vitro transcriptions require a linear dsDNA template (otherwise they will just continue transcribing whatever lies downstream of the terminator/polyA sequence).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7tqck","text":"Such a template can be produced in two ways:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"19fpu","text":"Linearizing a plasmid with a blunt-end cutting restriction enzyme as close as possible to the end of the SV40 polyA signal.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"755lo","text":"Amplifying the template by PCR.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ne35","text":"This protocol uses PCR generation of the template: it is more efficient and scalable in producing large amounts of template without requiring massive plasmid-prep endeavors and it allows easy addition of polyA tails via the reverse PCR primer. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ragh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a4pqi","text":"This protocol uses KOD hot-start high-fidelity polymerase for the PCR on pCR182 (as an example, it encodes an Aiptasia-optimized mNeonGreen) with a reverse primer not adding a polyA tail (but with the option to use one that adds a polyA tail of 50 bp to the template as would be recommended for Aiptasia), NEB DpnI for removing the circular template plasmid DNA and a Zymo Research DNA Clean \u0026 Concentrator®-5 Kit for concentrating and purifying the PCR product. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":19,"length":38},{"style":"bold","offset":306,"length":8},{"style":"bold","offset":368,"length":45},{"style":"italic","offset":310,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bkn4g","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"56pe9","text":"One 50-µL PCR reaction usually yields between 1 and 5 µg DNA, if larger amounts are needed, prepare multiple PCRs and use multiple columns for purification or use a purification kit with appropriate binding capacity in a single column. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"59t3j","text":"Before first time usage, ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":23}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9ud52","text":"It is recommended to split the dNTPs into smaller aliquots (50 or 100 µL) to prevent them from degrading during freeze-thaw cycles.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7b5ue","text":"The primers should be reconstituted to 100 µM in 10 mM Tris-Cl, pH 8.5 upon arrival. 10 µM working stocks can be prepared and stored at −20°C for multiple uses.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"167d9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d0v64","text":"Before every start:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2cvjk","text":"Thaw the plasmid DNA, primers, dNTPs, the 10× KOD buffer and the 25 mM MgSO4 and place on ice afterwards. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":75,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"41hr7","text":"Use standard best practices for molecular-biological work to prevent contamination.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8vpdq","text":"Before start, make yourself familiar with the whole protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

alternatively fluorescence-based DNA-quantification kit and detection device such as a Qubit™ fluorometer  or fluorescence microplate reader (not part of this protocol)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":6,"length":170}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"18i40","text":"Standard agarose-gel electrophoresis equipment and reagents.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ej8eo","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7br10","text":"Consumables and additional reagents:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":36}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f4f9k","text":"Standard 200-µL PCR tubes","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cfn9f","text":"Sterile 1.5-mL microcentrifuge tubes","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6qskq","text":"10 mM Tris-Cl, pH 8.5, nuclease-free (e.g., QIAGEN Elution Buffer EB - 19086)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"45gg1","text":"This is an EDTA-free alternative to the DNA elution buffer supplied in the Zymo Research Clean \u0026 Concentrator kit. "url":"https://www.zymoresearch.com/collections/dcc-pcr-purification-kits/products/dna-clean-concentrator-25"}}}}
steps: For mNeonGreen mRNA WITHOUT 50-nt polyA tail, prepare the following in a PCR tube (50 µL total): 32 µL nuclease-free water 5 µL 10 × KOD hot-start buffer 3 µL MgSO 4 (25 mM) 1.5 µL Pcmv_end_seq_fwd (10 µM) 1.5 µL SV40_PolyA_rev (10 µM) - not adding polyA tail 5 µL dNTPs (2 mM each) 1 µL pCR182 DNA (~50 ng/µL) from a dam + E. coli strain 1 µL KOD Hot-Start Polymerase It is crucial that the plasmid DNA was isolated from a dam + E. coli strain, this is what allows to selectively degrade the circular plasmid DNA with Dpn I after the successful PCR. Most routine cloning strains like DH5α, DH10B, TOP10, Mach1 and XL-1 blue are dam + . Examples of dam − strains are JM110 and SCS110 (this is their main feature). For mNeonGreen mRNA WITH 50-nt polyA tail, prepare the following PCR mix (50 µL total): 32 µL nuclease-free water 5 µL 10 × KOD hot-start buffer 3 µL MgSO 4 (25 mM) 1.5 µL Pcmv_end_seq_fwd (10 µM) 1.5 µL SV40_PolyAdT50_rev (10 µM) - adding 50-bp polyA tail 5 µL dNTPs (2 mM each) 1 µL pCR182 DNA (~100 ng/µL) 1 µL KOD Hot-Start Polymerase
Mix thoroughly by flicking the tube or pipetting (with a pipette set to 10-20 µL).
Spin down quickly (5 s) to collect liquid at the bottom and remove any possible air bubbles.

Place tube(s) in a PCR cycler and use the following PCR program: 1: 3 min 95°C initial denaturation -----35 cycles----- 2: 20 s 95°C denaturation 3: 20 s 60°C annealing 4: 20 s 70°C elongation -------------------- 5: 5 min 70°C final elongation 6: keep 4°C The PCR product for pCR182 with the specified primers is 1061 bp long KOD polymerase is really sensitive to extensive elongation time, so keep it close to what the user manual recommends No changes are required when using the SV40_PolyAdT50_rev reverse primer for a polyA tail Adjust cycle parameters when using a different DNA polymerase or template/primer combinations

Run 3 µL PCR reaction on a 1% agarose gel with your standard protocol (include an appropriate DNA ladder - load 0.5 µg). This should result in a strong, clear band at slightly above 1000 bp (see example image), slight background in the lane especially above the correct band might be a result of overloading the gel when the PCR worked well, that's fine.

Add 1 µL Dpn I (20 U/µL) to the PCR reaction, mix and incubate for 1 h at 37°C to selectively digest the dam-methylated plasmid DNA template. DpnI works fine in just the KOD PCR buffer (as well as Phusion and Q5 buffers), there is no need to add extra restriction enzyme buffer.
Add 250 µL (5× the sample volume) DNA Binding Buffer to the sample and mix thoroughly.
Transfer sample into Zymo-Spin™ Column in a collection tube.
Transfer Dpn I-treated PCR reaction into a 1.5-mL microcentrifuge tube. Keep PCR reaction on ice until proceeding with the next step.
Spin for 30 s at 16,000 ×g at RT. Discard flow-through.
Add 200 µL DNA Wash Buffer, centrifuge for 30 s at 16,000 ×g at RT. Discard flow-through.
Repeat go to step #11 one more time.

Add 6 µL 50°C-warm 10 mM Tris-Cl, pH 8.5 directly to the column matrix. The supplied DNA Elution Buffer can also be used but contains 0.1 mM EDTA which can interfere with enzymatic reactions if a relatively large volume of the PCR product needs to be used.
Centrifuge for 30 s at 16,000 ×g at RT.
Add another 6 µL 50°C-warm 10 mM Tris-Cl, pH 8.5 directly to the column matrix. This second elution can minimally improve the total yield at the cost of a more dilute sample.
Incubate at RT for 5 min.
OPTIONAL: Centrifuge empty column for 30 s at 16,000 ×g at RT. This just ensures that there are no traces of the wash buffer left inside or outside of the column.
Place the column into a sterile 1.5-mL microcentrifuge tube.
Centrifuge for 30 s at 16,000 ×g at RT.
Store DNA at −20°C.

OPTIONAL: Run 0.5-1 µL purified PCR reaction on a 1% agarose gel with your standard protocol (include an appropriate DNA ladder - load 0.5 µg). This should result in a strong, clear band at slightly above 1000 bp (see example image), slight background in the lane especially above the correct band might be a result of overloading the gel when the PCR worked well, that's fine. This is just for the paranoid (or after making a mistake during the purification). If the first gel directly after the PCR looked good and the NanoDrop shows the expected concentration, everything should be fine.

Quickly vortex the tube, then spin it down (5 s) and use 1 µL to measure the DNA concentration and quality on a NanoDrop blanked with 10 mM Tris-Cl, pH 8.5 (the same buffer used for elution). For a single 50-µL PCR eluted in 12 µL total, the concentration should be in the range between 80 and 400 ng/µL. The A 260 /A 280 should be ≥1.8, A 260 /A 230 should be ≥2.0
creator: Christian CR Renicke
